_comment: Engaged-soldier highlight demo (Settings.show_engaged_highlight, dev/debug visual): reuses anti-cav-square.json's rear-cavalry-charge setup (a lone player Infantry unit squares up against two cavalry charging its rear) as its own copy rather than editing the shared anti-cav-square.json in place, since that file is also reused by demo.380.json and website/tools/record-demos.sh's 'square' entry -- turning on a debug overlay there would change what those unrelated demos show. Requests the highlight via the session-only 'show_engaged_highlight' script field (Settings.set_show_engaged_highlight_session, DemoInputRecorder.gd), never persisted. The clip runs well past the point casualties start (~tick 240 in the underlying scenario), so the amber highlight is shown tracking the LIVE ring as soldiers die and the per-soldier array compacts -- the exact case UnitFormation.live_perimeter_indices fixes.
